result: At the quiet edge of dawn, I like to imagine the world holding its breath. Streetlights blink with the patience of sentries, and the birds test their voices like musicians warming up backstage. In those minutes before the sun commits to the horizon, color becomes a rumor whispered across the sky, telling stories about mountains I have never climbed and rivers I have never named. I make tea and listen for the soft click of the kettle, a tiny metronome measuring the passage from night to day. The steam curls upward, rehearsing the moves it will later teach to fog and cloud. While I wait, I open a notebook that has collected more intentions than sentences. The blank lines stare back, neither forgiving nor demanding, merely ready. I write slow words about the way light hesitates at the rim of the window, about the invisible choreography of dust motes stirred by the first breeze. Somewhere, cars are already exhaling exhaust, calendars are snapping shut, inboxes are flooding. Yet in my small kitchen, time folds like origami cranes and perches silently beside the fruit bowl. I think about friends scattered across time zones, each wrestling their own dawns, or perhaps their midnights, or perhaps the humming fluorescent glow of a sleepless office. I wonder if they feel the same mix of hope and apprehension when confronting a blank day. The kettle clicks again, reminding me that water cannot wait forever. I pour, sip, and let the warmth become a promise today.

prompt: Write freely about whatever you want for 250 words.
